(Rare). Rājā mārī podnī, tau bair bisāvan jāëṅ . Kahānī. The hen-bird by the Rājā slain, the cock-bird takes the field amain. soṅṭh bisā'nā , v. a. To purchase anything valuable or precious. Chār dinā kī āīyāṅ, aur soṅṭh bisāhan jāīyāṅ! E. Prov. But four days married and she's off to buy dry ginger (for her accouchement )!

more: n. m. S. विसर्ग ( Gram .) The sign (:); an aspirate. bisarg-sandhi , n. f. ( Sans. Gram .) The union of visarg with a vowel and a consonant (according to special rules) for the formation of a compound. Bate's Dictionary .

def: v.n. To be forgotten; to forget, lose the remembrance (of); to be unmindful (of).

v. n. To forget, to be forgotten.

def: v.t. To cause to be forgotten; to make (one) forget; to efface, or dismiss, from the memory, to forget ( = bisrānā).
more: v. a. S. विस्मरणं from वि neg. स्मृ to remember. To forget; efface or dismiss from the memory. Aise gur par tan, man wārūṅ, Rām tajūṅ, aru gur na bisārūṅ . Bhajan. A priest so true that for him mind and body I would stake, Even Rām give up, but naught should make me e'er my priest forsake.
(trans. of بسرنا) v. a. To forget.

n. m. S. विशेष from वि and शिष् to distinguish. 1. Difference; unlikeness; diversity; distinction. Shokhī, sharārat, makr o fan, sab kā bisekhā hai yahāṅ, Jo jo dikhāyā aur ko woh āp bhī dekhā yahāṅ . Nazīr. Insult and hurt, deceit and fraud, All have fit retribution known, Whatever one to others showed, Here to himself too has been shown. 2. Special property; peculiarity; habit ( bān ). In nainan kā yehī bisekh, Yeh bhī dekhā, woh bhī dekh . Prov. These eyes endowed are with this special bent, Now this one seen, on that too now intent. 3. The greater number; the majority. 4. ( In law ). A special rule or distinction. 5. ( In Med .) A change for the better. bisekh', basekh , adj. 1. Special; peculiar; particular; distinctive. 2. Superior; prime; excellent. bisekh, yā bashesh kar-ke , adv. 1. Particularly; specially. 2. (Wom.) About; on the point of. Marne basekh paṛā hai .
more: s. m. 1. Particular circumstance. 2. adj. Peculiar, special, particular, abundant. 3. adv. Particlarly, specially See بشيش.

n. m. 1. A medicinal plant; Trianthemā pentandrā . It spreads over the ground, and forms a circle of nearly a yard in diameter. 2. An animal of the lizard kind, said to be venomous. bis kī gāṅṭh, bis kī khān, puṛyā, yā poṭ , A viper; a demon; a villain of the deepest dye, hence a mischievous person; a little devil. 1. Mīṭhī bātoṅ pe na jā bis kī hai woh gāṅṭh, muā, Kyā kahūṅ, us se jo sadme mujhe, guīyāṅ, pahuṅhche! Wom. Jān Sāhab. Put nor your trust in honeyed words which poison's essence are, Such strokes as I have borne, my dear, death take the wretch nor spare! 2. Baṅgāle mat jāwe, pī, jahāṅ bis kī khān, Nār wahāṅ kī popnī, nāhak jāëgī jān . E. Play. Chaubolā . To Bengal go not, love, 'tis poison's deadly quarry true, The women fatal sorcerers are, in vain your end you 'll rue. bis milā'nā , v. a. lit . To mix poison. To spoil; corrupt. Maiṅ ne kyā bis milā dīyā thā? Prov. Saying I mixed no poison in your cup.
s.m. See s.v. bis. s.m. Poison, venom, bane ( = zahr); the vegetable poison Aconitum ferox (syn. baćnāg); (met.), anything bitter, disagreeable, spiteful, &c.

def: adj. For sacrifice; for slaughter.
intj. God be with you! God speed you! very good! very well! all right!
more: Mah. Cor. of ba-ism-i-Allāh, lit . In the name of God. 1. A phrase generally used by pious Mohamedans in the beginning of all actions or lessons, and especially before eating. 1. Pahle hī bismillah galat . Prov. Wrong at the outset. 2. Har niwāle bismillāh . Prov. At every mouthful it's "please God, I will." ☞ Said to one who is always ready to take all he can get, but very unwilling to work. 2. A ceremony performed at a Persian school ( maktab ) when a new boy is set to read for the first time. bismillah ! intj. Fac. bis milā , mix poison (in the cup). 1. Heaven preserve us! God bless you! Har qadam pe bismillāh . Mah. At every step it's 'God be with you'. (Fac.). Bāt bāt meṅ bis milāte haiṅ . Hin. At every turn you poison find. 2. Very good! very well! all right! ready! aye ready! Bismīllah! Mah. Ans. Bis milāne-wāle kā satyānās . Hin. bismil-lāhir-rahmān-nir-rahīm . In the name of God…

In the name of God, or بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم bismi-l-lāhi-r-rahmāni-r-raḥīm, In the name of God the most merciful. (An invocation generally used by Muhammadans at the beginning of all actions and works). بسم الله کرنا bismi-l-lāh karnā, To begin, to commence. بسم الله هوا bismi-l-lāh hūʼā, Said of a child who has just commenced learning to read.
subs: In the name of God the Merciful, the Compassionate
v.n. To pronounce the formula 'in the name of God,' at the beginning of a work, etc.; (hence) to begin, commence; to fall to (eating), set to work; to slaughter (an animal)
v.n. To abide under the dome of security, live in peace and safety; to retire from the world, lead a life of seclusion
'A beginning has been made at his lessons' (said of a child who has just commenced learning to read).

def: part. Amazed, &c. = bismayī, q.v. adj. Astonished, taken by surprise, amazed, disconcerted, perplexed, bewildered, dismayed, afraid.

adj. & s.m. Vicious, dissolute, addicted to any kind of vice or evil practice; delicate, nice; showy in dress (syn. sughaṛ); — dissolute man, voluptuary, libertine, debauchee, rake, roué, lecher (cf. bishnī).
more: 1. s. m. A rake, a debauchee, a lecher. 2. adj. Delicate, nice, showy in dress.

def: adj. Free from grief or sorrow, happy.
s.m. Twenty days of an intercalary month (for which it is the custom among bohrās or money-lenders to cut off the interest); the interest so cut off.
adj. Forgetful, oblivious; negligent.
s.f. Forgetfulness, &c. = bis̤ar, q.v. s.m. Poison, venom, bane ( = zahr); the vegetable poison Aconitum ferox (syn. baćnāg); (met.), anything bitter, disagreeable, spiteful, &c.
subs: v.n. lit. 'To vomit venom'; to say venomous or spiteful things (of), speak ill (of), malign, revile, sneer at; to disburden the mind or the conscience; to have (one's) say; to take revenge
adj. & s.m. Poisonous; a venomous serpent; snake
v.n. To swallow or take poison

bis-khoprā (S. visha+kharpara+kah), s.m. lit. 'Poison-headed'; the medicinal plant Trianthema pentandra, or T. obcordatum; the plant Mezoneurum cucullatum (Desfontaines), or Cæsalpinia cucullata (Roxb.); — a species of lizard, about half a yard long, which is generally regarded as venomous, Lacerta iguana
bis-khān, bis-gāṅṭh, s.m.f. Viper, mischievous person, devil, demon
(-meṅ), To mingle poison (with), to poison, embitter, corrupt, vitiate, spoil

def: s.m. Rest, repose, quiet, ease, cessation from labour or fatigue; pause, stop, point in writing
more: n. m. S. विश्राम from वि apart, श्रम् to labour. 1. Rest; repose; ease; comfort. 1. Rām kāj kīne binā, mohe kahāṅ bisrām, Daë asīs Sursā chalī, harakh chale Hanūmān . Rāmāyan. 2. Tan takyā, aur man bisrām, Jahāṅ paṛ rahe, vahīṅ ārām . Prov. The body is a hermit's cell, the mind the tenant is, Where'er it chance to lie there rest that comes to all is his. 2. A pause; a stop; a point in writing. bisrām lenā, yā kar'nā , v. n. To rest; take rest; repose; lie down; sleep. Ham pardesī pāohne, ān kīyo bisrām, Bhor bhaë uṭh jāëṅge, baso tihāro gām! Dohā. A stranger and a guest I came here and found rest, At dawn I'll go, your village be for ever blest!
s. m. Rest, ease, repose, pause, a stop, a point in writing. بسرام لينا bisrām lenā, To rest, to repose, to pass the night (particularly used by wandering faḳīrs ).
subs: bisrām lenā, v.n. To rest, repose, &c.; to pass the night (particularly used by wandering faqīrs).

def: s.m. = bisrām, q.v. s.m. Rest, repose, quiet, ease, cessation from labour or fatigue; pause, stop, point in writing
subs: bisrām lenā, v.n. To rest, repose, &c.; to pass the night (particularly used by wandering faqīrs).

def: v.t. To cause to forget; to forget ( = bisārnā), to efface from the memory; to distract, beguile; to mislead (syn. bhulānā; bahkānā).
more: v. a. To efface from the memory; to distract (the senses.) 1. Aisī bansī bajāī, morī sudh bisrāī . Song. 2. Kab se rājā īshar bhaë, Kodoṅ ke din bīsar gaë? Prov. Since when a mighty king grown to, your altered lot, The days you lived on porridge meal now all forgot.
(causal of بسرنا) v. n. To forget, to cause to forget, to mislead.

def: part. Rested, reposed.
part. adj. = bisrānt, q.v. part. Rested, reposed.
s.f. Rest, repose, &c. = bisrām, q.v. s.m. Rest, repose, quiet, ease, cessation from labour or fatigue; pause, stop, point in writing
subs: bisrām lenā, v.n. To rest, repose, &c.; to pass the night (particularly used by wandering faqīrs).

def: s.m. A provincial term (in Kamāʼūn) for a kind of taʻalluqdār whose office is in the gift of government.
adj. Twenty.
more: (for بيست) Twenty. bast, part. (in compos.) Bound, shut. bust, s. A rose-bower or a place abounding with sweet-smelling fruits.

def: s.m. Spreading out; spread, extension, expansion, diffusion, development, amplification, detail, prolixity, copiousness, abundance; (in Geom.) diameter of a circle
more: n. m. S. विस्तार from वि and स्मृ spread. 1. Extension; expansion; spread ( phailāo ); diffusion; enlargement; amplification; size. 1. Sat yojan samudr bistārā, Gayo Pawan-sut tāke pārā . Rāmāyan. 2. Ādhī kīch, tihāī jal, daswāṅ ans siwār, Bāwan gaz bāhar rahī, silā kitek bistār? Math. Problem. Ans. 780. 2. Abundance; copiousness; prolixity. 3. Amplification; detail; explanation; exposition. 4. ( Math .) The diameter of a circle ( qutr ).
s. m. Spreading out, abundance, copiousness, prolixity, diffusion.
subs: (-kā), To spread, extend, enlarge, expand, diffuse, develop, &c.

def: s.m. Bedding, mattress (cf. bistarā); carpet; bed, bolster, pillow.
more: n. m. S. स्तृ to spread. 1. A bedding ( bichhonā ); mattress; carpeting. 2. The abode or resting place of a faqīr. Kafan hamārī gūdṛī, gor hamāro gāoṅ, Lahad hamārā bistārā, khāk hamaro nāoṅ . Mah. Dohā. The winding sheet my covering, and the grave my hame, The washing board my bedding is, and dust my name. 3. A plant; Callicarpa Americanā. bistrā lagā'nā, bichhānā, yā jamānā , v. n. To spread a carpet; make the bed.
s. m. 1. Bedding, carpeting. 2. A bed, &c.

def: s.m. Bed, bedding (particularly of a soldier, faqīr, traveller, &c., which is rolled up in the morning); the abode or resting place of a faqīr (cf. takiya)
more: s. m. 1. A bed, &c. peculiar to faḳīrs. 2. The abode of a faḳīr.
subs: bistarā jamānā, bistarā lagānā, v.n. To spread or lay out the bedding, make ready the bed.

def: s.m. Bed, bedding (particularly of a soldier, faqīr, traveller, &c., which is rolled up in the morning); the abode or resting place of a faqīr (cf. takiya)
subs: bistarā jamānā, bistarā lagānā, v.n. To spread or lay out the bedding, make ready the bed.

def: adj. Extending, large, ample, diffuse.

v.t. To spread, extend, &c. = bistār karnā, q.v.s.v. bistār. s.m. Spreading out; spread, extension, expansion, diffusion, development, amplification, detail, prolixity, copiousness, abundance; (in Geom.) diameter of a circle
more: v. a. To spread out; expand; enlarge; lengthen ( baṛhānā ).
v. a. To spread out, to extend, to diffuse.
subs: (-kā), To spread, extend, enlarge, expand, diffuse, develop, &c.

def: s.f. The house-lizard (sy. n ćhipkalī; pallī).
s.f. The house-lizard (syn. ćhipkalī; pallī).
v.n. To sob, &c. = bisūrnā, q.v.
v.n. To put on a crying face, to prepare to weep; to cry slowly; to sob; to pull a long face.
more: v. n. Pr. visūra to be distressed. 1. To look as if about to weep; to be ready to cry. Nā mere paṅkh, na pāoṅ bal, maiṅ apaṅkh, pīyā dūr, Uṛ na sakūṅ, gir gir paṛūṅ, rahūṅ bisūr bisūr . No wings have I, nor strength of limb, Wingless, my husband far from me, I cannot fly, I drop and fall, And sob alone with misery. 2. To make a wry face; to be sulky. Pissū se, machchharoṅ se rove, koī bisūre . Nazīr. Fleas and musquitoes do the rains bring out, Some crying and some sobbing are about.
v. n. To cry slowly, to sob.

def: see بسوه biswa.
more: s. m. The universe, the world.

ety: Ap. Prk. वीसवँओ = Prk. वीसमओ = S. विंशम+कः
def: s.m. Twentieth part; a measure of land, the twentieth part of a bīghā
more: s. m. A land measure, the twentieth part of a بيگها bīghā. See بسوه.
subs: s.m., biswāṅsī, s.f. (i.e. biswā+ansa = S. anśa+kah), Twentieth part of a biswā
s.m. Collecting, or collection, by the biswā
s.m. Holder of a share or shares in a coparcenary village; — biswā-dārī, s.f. Coparcenary holding or tenure; tenure of independent village communities under a superior taʻallug-dār.

def: s.m. = biswā, q.v. see بسوه biswa.
more: n. m. H. bīs twenty. The twentieth part of a bīgah ; a land measure; a share of land. biswah-dār , n. m. The holder of a share or shares in a coparcenary village. bīs bisve ; Poet. bisve bīs , adv. lit . All the twenty biswās (in a bīgah ). Certainly; surely; no doubt; necessarily. Hon-hār miṭtī nahīṅ, hove bisve bīs . Prov. What is to be will not rub out, but surely be. das bisweh , n. m. Ten biswās or half a bīgah , hence a half share.
s. m. The twentieth part (particularly, of a بيگها bīghā ). See کٿها kaṭṭhā.

def: adj. & s.m. All, every, every one, entire, whole, universal; — whole world, all creation, universe
subs: s.m. lit. 'All-maker'; name of the artificer or architect of the gods, the Vulcan of Hindu mythology; the god of artisans (especially of carpenters); — an astute or crafty person.

def: s.m. See s.v. biswa. see بسوه biswa.
more: n. m. S. विश्वकर्म्मा A son of Brahma; a saint. 1. An artificer of the gods; the Indian Vulcan; the god of carpenters. 2. A crafty or mischievous person.
s. m. The artificer of the gods, the Indian vulcan.

def: adj. Twentieth
subs: adv. In the twentieth place, twentiethly.

def: adj. S. बिंश, Pr. bīso . Twentieth. bīsyoṅ, bīsoṅ , adj. Numberless; countless ( an-ginat ).
s.f. See s.v. biswā. see بسوه biswa.
more: n. f. The twentieth part of a Biswā .

def: s.m. (?), Mixture of spices and aromatic seeds powdered and ready for use in curry or pickle; curry-powder.
more: n. bīs twenty. A mixture of spices, etc. used in curries and pickles.

def: s.m. Trust, confidence, faith, belief, reliance, dependence
more: Hin. n. m. S. विश्वास, Pr. vissāso, vīsāso . 1. Faith; belief; confidence; trust; reliance; dependance. 2. (A. vasvās ). Doubt; suspicion. Illit. biswās karnā yā rakhnā , Hin. v. n. To believe; credit; rely upon; confide. Rām nām kahte raho, mat kar tan biswās . Ratnāvlī. On Rām's name ever call, nor of the body aught take thought at all. biswās-ghāt , n. f. Taking advantage of the confidence of others; a treacherous friendship ( yār-mārī ). biswās-ghātī , n. m. A treacherous friend ( yār-mār ); one who seeks to take advantage of the confidence placed in him.

s. m. Trust, confidence, faith. بسواس گهات biswās-ghāt, s. f. Taking advantage of the confidence of others, treacherous friendship. بسواسگهاتي biswās-ghāt-ī, s. m. A treacherous friend, one who seeks to take advantage of the confidence placed in him.
subs: biswās karnā (-kā; or -par), To put trust (in), to confide (in), rely (on), to trust, believe, credit
s.f. Destruction of confidence, breach of faith, violation of trust, treachery, treacherous friendship; — biswās-ghātī, s.m. One who betrays confidence, traitor, treacherous friend.

def: s.m. Corr. of A. waswās, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (f. biswāsin, -inī), Confiding, faithful, believing; honest, trusty; — truster, believer; confidant.
adj. Of or relating to the world, worldly, belonging to the universe.
more: n. f. Alienation of land on the payment of fines in advance.
adj. Worldly, relating to the world.

def: s.f. Alienation of land on low jamaʻs on the payment of fines in advance.
adj. Much, many, very, abundant.
adj. & s.m. = bisailā, q.v. adj. & s.m. Poisonous, venomous (syn. zahrīlā); — venomous reptile; snake; phlegmon or boil on the hand (bisahrī).
adj. Many, much, very, abundant.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بسیار (bisyâr, “much, many, very”), from Middle Persian [script needed] (wsy(k)ʾl /⁠wasyār⁠/, “much, many”).

def: s.f. Abundance, superfluity.
s.f. Grazing fee charged by herdsmen, demand on each head of cattle paid to the cowherd, a kind of agistment
more: n. f. Grazing fee charged by herdsmen; the demand on each head of cattle paid to the cowherd; a kind of agistment. Guāl-bīt jo de nāhīṅ, maryo us ke ḍhor, Ghar ghātan ko lūṭ-kar, le jāīṅ din māṅ chor! Hīṛo. Song. Cursed be the man who grazing fee withholds and will not pay! May all his cattle die, be sacked his house in open day!
n. f. S. विट. 1. The dung or excrement of birds. 2. A factitious salt containing sulphur.
subs: s.m. Field or land grazed on by agistment; — land cultivated by forced labour.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بیت (bayt), from Arabic بَيْت (bayt).

def: s.f. = bīṭh, q.v. s.f. Excrement, dung (especially of birds).
more: s. f. ( विटू ) 1. Dung of any animal. 2. (Same as विटलवण ) A factitious salt containing sulphur.

ety: S. विट, rt. विट्; or विड
def: s.f. A factitious salt containing sulphur ( = biṭ-lavaṇ).
s.f. = bīt, q.v. s.f. Grazing fee charged by herdsmen, demand on each head of cattle paid to the cowherd, a kind of agistment
subs: s.m. Field or land grazed on by agistment; — land cultivated by forced labour.

def: s.m. Span = bittā, q.v. s.m. Span, hand's breadth.
more: n. m. S. वटु a boy. 1. A son; boy; child; darling. 1. Jaisā sūt waisā pheṭā, jaisa bāp waisā beṭā . Prov. The cloth as the thread, the son as the sire. 2. Maiṅ to beṭā de-ke beṭī letā hūṅ . 2. A form of address used by Faqīrs towards their Chelās (disciples), etc. beṭā banānā , v. n. To adopt a son ( god lenā ). beṭā beṭī , n. m. Children; offspring; issue. Dunyā meṅ do hī chīz haiṅ, beṭā yā beṭī . Prov. Two blessings all this world affords, a daughter or a son accords. (Said to console the parents to whom a daughter has been born when they would rather have had a son). beṭe pote-wālā , n. m. beṭe pote-wālī , n. f. 1. A grandfather or grandmother. 2. A man or woman blest with children. beṭe-wālā , n. m. beṭe-wālī , n. f. The father or mother of the bridegroom. beṭā-khāū , n. m. A father who survives his son.

def: v.t. To spread, &c. = bithārnā, biṭānā, biṭlānā, q.q.v.; to put (food) into (the mouth), to taste (food); e.g. muṅh biṭālnā.
v. a. To taste (as food). Mūṅh biṭāl-ke jāiyo . Wom.

s.m. Awning, canopy, &c. = vi-tān, q.v. adj. Empty, vacant; pithless, sapless; dull, stupid; — wicked, abandoned; — s.m. Stretching, spreading out; expansion; — an awning, a canopy, cover; a cushion; — warp (of cloth); — an oblation, a sacrifice; the sacrificial hearth (on which the sacred fire is kept); — a heap, quantity; — season, opportunity.
v.t. To spread, &c. = bithrānā, biṭālnā, q.q.v. v.t. To spread, scatter, strew; to sprinkle; to bespatter; to splash, spill; to waste.
more: v. a. To scatter, to sprinkle.

def: v.t. To cause to pass, to pass, spend (time).
more: v. a. S. व्यतीत. To pass, spend (time) ( guzārnā ). Sakhī sab ghar ba-ghar baṅgle chhawāweṅ, Piyā ke saṅg meṅ nis din bitāweṅ . Song Bārā-māsā . Burnished and bright are all the houses round, In love's delights the maidens with their mates abound.
(trans of بتنا or بيتنا) v. a. To pass.
wety: From Sanskrit वत् (vat) + ـآ (-ā, causative augment) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix).

def: s.m. Young branch of a tree, new shoot or sprout; branch; tree, bush; clump, cluster, tuft; — spreading, expansion.

s.m. Spreading, extension, &c. = vi-stār, q.v. s.m. Spreading; spread, extension, expansion, diffusion, development (i.q. bistār); — breadth, amplitude; vastness, expanse; — copiousness, abundance; — particularizing at length, detail, amplification, prolixity; — diameter (of a circle); — the branch of a tree with its new shoots; a shrub
subs: adv. In detail, at length, fully, &c.

def: s.m. Giving up, abandoning; donation, gift, charity, alms.
more: s. m. Giving charity, donation, alms.

def: v.t. To give away, bestow, grant.
s.m. Little son, &c. = biṭwā, q.v.
s.m. = biṭaurā, q.v.
more: n. m. A heap of cakes of dried cow-dung plastered on the outside to protect them from rain. 1. Biṭauṛe meṅ se uple hī nikleṅge . Prov. From the dung-heap only cakes of dung can come. 2. Sūjhe na biṭauṛā, chāṅd se "Rām Rām ." Prov. He cannot see the dung-heap before his nose, and the new moon he salutes "Rām Rām." 3. Girgaṭ kī dauṛ biṭauṛā . Prov. The lizard's refuge is the dung-heap. Slang. 4. But, biṭauṛā banā baiṭhā hai . He sits as senseless as a statue or a dung-heap.

def: s.m. Heap of cakes of dried cowdung (used for fuel).
more: s. m. A heap of the cakes of dried cow-dung.

def: s.f. A tax on shopkeepers, &c. in a village.
s.f. Excrement, dung (especially of birds).
s.f. Disquietude, agitation; pain, agony; anguish, ache; distress, misery, trouble.
s.m. Annular cushion put on the head to carry a pot of water, &c. (syn. bīṅḍā; inḍwā).
more: s. m. An annular cushion put on the head to carry a pot of water on.

def: n. f. S. व्यथा 1. A sad story; a tale of misery; a tragedy. Bāle-pan kī suno bithā, jis din dhartī līyā janam, Sunat pitā jī mukh morin, aur sust hue sab log kuṭam . Song. Lāonī . The story of my childhood, hear, the day that I had birth, My father turned his face aside, gloom came upon our hearth. (Disappointment at the birth being a girl). 2. Hardship; affliction; misfortune ( āfat ).
s.m. Ant-hill.
v.n. To be alarmed, scared, &c. = bijhaknā, q.v. v.n. To be startled, scared, frightened; to start; to take fright (cf. jhijhaknā, bidakna, bhićaknā).
v.t. = biṭhānā, q.v. (cf. also the forms biṭhlānā, baiṭhānā, baiṭhālnā).

v. a. S. उपविष्ट from उपवशि् to sit down. 1. To locate; station; pitch. 2. To set; place; seat; lay; stow; deposit. Anḍoṅ pe biṭhānā . 3. To make a child sit on a pot de chambre , or go to stool. Wom. Bachche ko biṭhā le, nahīṅ kapṛe bharegā . Wom. 4. To make one sit down; give one a seat. Barābar biṭhānā . 5. To lodge; post; house; install. Yeh do dil ko ek jā biṭhātā nahīṅ, Kisī kā ise vasl bhātā nahīṅ . Mīr Hasan. Two hearts the envious sky ne'er brings together, It likes not happy unions with each other. 6. To appoint a watch; place on guard. Apne māl pe ādmī biṭhāo . 7. To set, fix, stick in; insert (a stone) ( jaṛnā ). Nagīnā biṭhānā . 8. To thrust in; drive in or home ( utārnā ). Pech biṭhānā . 9. To plant; sow ( lagānā ). Paudā biṭhānā . 10. To put in its proper place; bring in. Maiṅ ne Amjad Alī Shāh kī jagah Wājid Alī Shāh ko biṭhā dīyā . * Gālib. 11. To enter one (in a school, or shop). 12. To establish; fix; appoint; institute; constitute ( muqarrar k .). Jāgīr meṅ kisī ne zar-rez mulk pāyā, Kar band-o-bast apnā nazm o nasaq biṭhāyā . Nazīr. 13. To assess; levy; impose ( lagānā ). Ṭikas biṭhānā . 14. To settle one in business; bind (an apprentice). Sau rupaë de-ke use biṭhā dīyā . 15. To enthrone; install; raise to the throne. Takht pe biṭhānā . 16. To inculcate; impress; imbue; instill; inspire. Aisā bhaë hogā parjā ko, aisā dāb baṭhāwegā, Chor, jār, gunḍā, baṭmārā ḍhūṅḍā nāhīṅ pāwegā . W. Rus. Song on the Prince of Wales. Such fear and awe he 'll strike that though they now abound, Thieves, rakes, roués, and ṭhags , tho' sought will ne'er be found. 17. To make one believe ( yaqīn dilānā ); to influence. Dil meṅ biṭhānā. Jāne uske dil meṅ kyā biṭhā dīyā? 18. To keep as a mistress or kept-woman. Ranḍī ko ghar meṅ biṭhā līyā . 19. To keep at home (a marriageable or married daughter); to refuse to give in marriage. Apn/?/ bachchī ko biṭhā rakhtī, na tum ko detī, Jān Sāhab, maiṅ agar jāntī aīyāsh tumheṅ . Wom. Jān Sāhab. 20. To gather; collect; realize ( ugāhnā ). 21. To cause to be assembled; convene ( ikhaṭṭā k .); convoke. Panchāyat biṭhānā . 22. To cause to congeal; solidify; harden. Pārā biṭhānā . 23. To cause to be formed into lumps. Chāṅwal biṭhā dīye . 24. To cause to sink or fall in. Ek ghūṅse meṅ āṅkh biṭhā dī . 25. To stuff ( ṭhūsnā ); press (cotton etc.). 26. To reduce; bring down; contract; condense. Das ser dūdh meṅ do ser kundā biṭhāyā . Halwāīs. 27. To level; plane; make flat, even. Ṭhok-pīṭ-ke biṭhā dīyā . 28. To bring down; destroy; demolish ( ḍhānā ). Meṅh ne ghar biṭhā dīyā . 29. To melt; dissolve; make sloppy or thin. Barsāt ne sārā guṛ biṭhā dīyā . 30. To cause to sink or founder ( ḍubānā ). Tūfān ne jahāz biṭhā dīyā . 31. To ruin; destroy; bring to nought; do for (a person). Ek hī sadme ne biṭhā dīyā . 32. To cause the heart to sink; to break one's heart. Dil biṭhānā . 33. To stun; to cause to faint. Ek ghūṅse meṅ biṭhā dīyā . 34. To set in order; arrange; adjust; fix. 35. To proportion; suit; adapt; dove-tail. Joṛ biṭhānā . 36. To calculate; compute; strike a balance ( phailānā ). 1. Hisāb biṭhānā . 2. Mīzān biṭhānā . 37. To bring out a result; make out; produce. Ser kā savā ser biṭhāyā . 38. To hazard; chance; stake; bet with or upon ( aṛnā ). (Gamblers). Dāoṅ pe biṭhā dīyā . 39. To pledge; pawn; mortgage ( girvī dharnā ). (Gamblers). 40. To gamble away ( hārnā ). (Gamblers). Sārā māl juë meṅ biṭhā dīyā .

v.t. To cause to enter; to enter, place (in, -meṅ, as a boy in a school); to pierce, penetrate, thrust in, drive home, press down, ram down, pack closely, stuff, fix firmly; — to cause to be seated, cause to sit, to seat; to settle; to set (as a stone in a ring, &c.), to plant, sow; to cause to congeal, coagulate, solidify; to cause to cake, form into lumps; to locate, station, lodge; to instal; to establish, fix, set up; to cause to settle down; to lay (as dust, &c.), to allay, calm, assuage; to set (over, -par, as a watch, guard, &c.); to fix or impress (on, -meṅ, the mind), to implant (in the mind), impress (the mind with an idea), strike (terror, &c. into the mind); to set in order, settle, arrange, adjust; to make out (a calculation, &c.), strike (a balance); to establish, get up, cause to be convened (a panćāyat, &c.); to come into possession of, gain, realize, gather, collect; to make (the heart) sink or faint (dil biṭhānā)
more: (caus. of بيٿهنا) v. a. To cause to sit down, to seat, to set, to settle.
subs: v.t. (intensive of biṭhānā, but often used in the sense of biṭhānā), To cause to fall in, cause to sink or founder; to demolish, destroy, raze, level, ruin; to melt, dissolve; to settle, knock down, stun, &c. (with, -meṅ, a blow); to stake, bet (upon, -par); to gamble away, lose
v.t. To keep seated; to detain; to keep (a marriageable or married daughter) at home; to refuse to give in marriage; — biṭhā-lenā, v.t. To make (one) sit = biṭhānā (generally in an intensive sense).

def: s.f. Brushing or cleansing the warp ( = bithrī).

ety: bithār˚ = Prk. वित्थारे(इ) = S. विस्तारय(ति), caus. of rt. वि+स्तृ
def: v.t. To spread, scatter, &c. ( = bithrānā, q.v.; and see also bitharnā, of which it is the caus.).
more: (caus. of بتهرنا) v. a. To cause to scatter, to cause to sprinkle.

def: v.n. To be spread, sown, scattered; to be sprinkled, splashed, &c.
more: Poet. v. n. To be scattered ( bikharnā ); to spread. Bāl hain bithre, āṅkh unīdī, Shām savere utṭhe rī . Song. Brij. Shām early rose with hair dishevelled and a liquid langour in his eyes.
(perhaps from विस्तरण r. स्तृ Spread over, cover) v. n. To be scattered, to be sprinkled, to be spread.

def: v.t. To cause to be spread, &c.
s.f. Row, line; road; terrace in front of a house; stall, shop; — a sort of drama; a division of the planetary spheres (comprising three asterisms).

more: (caus. of بيٿهنا) v. a. To cause to sit down, to seat, to set, to settle.
subs: v.t. (intensive of biṭhānā, but often used in the sense of biṭhānā), To cause to fall in, cause to sink or founder; to demolish, destroy, raze, level, ruin; to melt, dissolve; to settle, knock down, stun, &c. (with, -meṅ, a blow); to stake, bet (upon, -par); to gamble away, lose
v.t. To keep seated; to detain; to keep (a marriageable or married daughter) at home; to refuse to give in marriage; — biṭhā-lenā, v.t. To make (one) sit = biṭhānā (generally in an intensive sense).

def: v.n. (local) = baiṭhnā, q.v. v.n. To seat oneself, sit down, be seated, be unemployed or idle; to sit, brood, incubate; to alight, settle, perch; to ride (on, -par); to subside, settle, abate; to pair, couple (a bird); to obtain employment or service; to be laid out or expended (on, -par); to visit a person in grief for the purpose of condolence; to sit in dharnā (q.v.)
more: v. n. (dakh.) To sit: see بيٿهنا baiṭhnā.
subs: v.n. To sit down; to settle down; to fall in or down (a house, wall, &c.).

def: v.t. To spread, scatter, strew; to sprinkle; to bespatter; to splash, spill; to waste.
more: Poet. v. a. S. विस्तरण from स्तृ to spread over. To waste; scatter ( bakhernā ); throw about; spill ( khinḍānā ). Kuchh khāyā, kuchh bithrāyā . Song.
v. a. To scatter, to sprinkle.

def: s.f. = bithārī, q.v. s.f. Brushing or cleansing the warp ( = bithrī).
E. n. f. Cleansing the warp.
part. adj. Passed, elapsed, gone, spent, bygone
more: adj. S. व्यतीत Passed; gone; elapsed; bygone. Barsan bhaë batīt, pīyā ke darshan ko rī . Song.

def: v.n. To pass, elapse, &c. ( = bitīt honā, bītnā); to happen (to), to betide.
more: v. n. To pass; elapse; go by.

def: s.f. Little daughter; daughter.
more: (dimin. of بيٿي) s. f. A daughter.

def: s.m. Black salt, a particular kind of fetid salt (obtained by fusing fossile salt with emblic myrobalans, and used medicinally).
s.m. A coarse species of pulse (of the māsh, or of the kalāya kind).
more: s. A coarse species of pulse of the kalāya kind: see S̱. کلاٴي kalāʼī.

def: v.n. To pass; to come to pass, &c. = bītnā, q.v.
more: v. a. S. बट् to divide. 1. To realize. Kaho āj kyā baṭṭā? 2. To share; gain; obtain (as a profit). Rupaë ke saude meṅ chār paise baṭṭe .

v. n. 1. To be divided, shared, distributed. 2. To be dispersed, scattered, separated. Khawāseṅ jo thīṅ rū-barū, haṭ gaīṅ. Bahāne se har kām ke baṭ gaīṅ . Mīr Hasan. 3. To be distracted, drawn off (attention). Dhyān baṭnā .
v. a. S. वट् to surround. To twist; to form by convolution (thread).
n. m. An instrument with which ropes are twisted.
v. n. To come to pass.

ety: bīt˚ = Prk. वित्ते(इ), or वित्त(इ), fr. वित्त = S. वीत, p.p.p. rt. वी
def: v.n. To pass, elapse, expire (as time); to come to pass, take place, occur, happen, befall, arrive; to undergo, suffer, endure
more: v. n. S. ब्यातीत 1. To pass over or away; elapse; go by; run out; expire; to be past and gone. 1. Bītī tāhi bisār de, āge kī sudh leu . Girdhar Kuṅdlī. Forget the past, regard the future. 2. Kalyāṅ man meṅ sochat haiṅ, aur phūl khile kumlāvat haiṅ, Jo din un par bīt gaë, ab so din ham par āwat haiṅ . Dohā. The young buds in their minds revolve the full-blown flowers' decay, The evil fate which these assails will come on us one day. 2. To take place; befal; happen; occur; come to pass; come about. 3. To feel; suffer; bear; endure. Jis pe bitī ho ye wohī jāne . Jān Sāhab.
v. n. To pass, come to pass, happen, befall: see بتنا bitnā.
subs: v.n. To pass away, pass over, &c.

ety: bīṭ˚ = Prk. विट्टे(इ), or विट्ट(इ), fr. विट्ट (for विट्ठ) = S. व्यस्त, p.p.p. of rt. वि+अस
def: v.t. To pour out, spill; to scatter.
more: v. n. To spill, to scatter.

def: s.f. = bhiṭnī, q.v. s.f. Nipple (of a woman's breast).
v.t. To scatter, &c. = biṭānā, q.v. (cf. the forms ḍubānā and ḍubonā).
more: 1. v. a. To scatter, to sprinkle. 2. s. m. A son: see بٿوا biṭawā.

def: s.f. Detailed settlement = bila-bandī, q.v. s.f. An account of the settlement of a district (specifying the name of each maḥal, the farmer of it, and the amount at which it is let, &c.).
v.t. To bespatter, &c. ( = bithrānā, q.v.); to asperse, calumniate, vilify, denounce; to belie.
more: v. a. 1. To find fault; pick holes ( aib nikālnā ). Bhale chaṅge kām ko bitrātā hai . 2. To impute falsehood ( jhuṭlānā ); deny; belie. Mujh ko bitrātā hai .

def: s.m. Wealth, riches, property, possessions, money, substance, thing; power, ability, means, resources
more: + Wom. n. f. S. वित्तम power. 1. Means ( bisāt ); ability; power ( bal ); reach (pauṅhch); resources. Dān bit samān . Prov. Let your charity be as your means. 2. Money; riches; wealth; substance. 3. Bulk; magnitude; size; height ( uṅchāī ). Ek bittā kā bit nū, nau bittā kī ḍāḍhī . Prov. Not a span high, and a beard nine spans long! bit bāhar , adj. Out of one's power.
subs: adj. Out of (one's) power, beyond (one's) means.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بت (bat, “mark, score”).

def: s.m. Span, hand's breadth.
more: n. m. S. वितस्ति A span; a hand's breadth. bit'tyā , n. m. A span high ( bālishtiyā ); a dwarf.

def: adv. Explicitly, in detail, &c. ( = biʼt-taṣrīḥ); minutely, item by item, particularly.
more: adv. Particularly; at full length; item by item.

def: adv. Especially, particularly, specifically.
adv. Little by little, bit by bit, piecemeal, in detail; by degrees; gradually.
adv. In detail; expressly, distinctly, explicitly.
more: adv. In detail; expressly; explicitly; distinctly.

def: s.f. A boys' game (a boy throws forward a small round potsherd with his foot, which another boy has to bring back with his breath held, and crying bittī, bittī); a run.
more: n. f. H. bittā span. A boys' game, so called from bittī a small round potsherd held under the great toe and thrown forward. This potsherd has to be brought back by one of the competitors with his breath held all the time, crying bittī bittī . Hence, a run ( dauṛ ). Jo hār gaë, to sau bittiyāṅ leṅge . If you fail the penalty is a hundred runs.
n. f. A pair; couple; match ( joṛ ). biṭ'ṭī budnā , v. n. To couple; match; form pairs.

def: s.f. Pair, couple, match
subs: v.n. To couple, match, form pairs.

def: s.m. The melon ( = ḵẖarbuza); the water-melon, Cucurbita citrullus.
s.m. lit. 'A span high'; a dwarf (syn. bālishtiyā).
more: s. m. A dwarf (i. e. a span high).

ety: S. वटु+कः; or dim. of beṭā
def: s.m. Little son; dear son; little boy, child.
v.t. To cause to be measured; to have (one) measured or fitted.
more: Mah. v. a. Caus. of بيونتنا q. v.
v. a. Caus. of بتنا q. v.

def: s.m. Marriage ( = byāh, q.v. s.m. Marriage
subs: s.f. The marriage fees of a Brāhmaṇ
byāh kar-denā (-se), To marry (one, to), to marry or take a wife (e.g. ćār byāh kiyā, he took four wives); to celebrate a marriage
byāh lenā, byāh le-ānā, byāh le-jānā (-ko), To marry, take in marriage, bring home a wife

def: s. f. A kibe, blister or chap on the foot, chilblain.
more: s. f. Sowing, the act of sowing, seedtime.

more: n. f. S. विपादिका A kibe or chap on the heel, generally caused by cold; a chilblain. Jiske na phaṭī biwāī, woh kyā jāne pīṛ parāī? Prov. Who never in his heel a chap did feel, To him another's pain did ne'er appeal. bivāī phaṭnā , v. n. To suffer from chilblains.
subs: v.n. To suffer from kibes, or from chilblains.

def: s.m. = bimān, q.v. s.m. Car or chariot of the gods (sometimes serving as a seat or throne, and at others carrying them through the skies self-directed and self-moving); car or vehicle (generally); ornamental bier (on which a Hindū corpse is borne to the place of cremation).
s.f. Judicial decree according to Hindū law ( = bewasthā; fatwā).
s.f. Lady ( = bībī, q.v.); an epithet of Fātima, the daughter of Mohammad (to whom Mohammadan women make offerings which none but a chaste woman dare touch).
more: n. f. 1. See بي بي 2. Fātimah , the daughter of Mahommed to whom Mah. women make offerings which none but a chaste woman dare touch. bīvī kā dānā khāne-wālā n. m. One who lives on the income or earnings of his wife.
s. f. (dakh.) Lady ( Harris ): see بي بي bībī.

def: direct plural of بِیوی (bievī, “wife”)
oblique plural of بِیوی (bievī, “wife”)
vocative plural of بِیوی (bievī, “wife”)
A mode of calling pigeons.
(imperat. of āmadan), Come
more: n. m. S. वीज seed. 1. Seed. 2. A person appointed to measure out grain. bayāī , n. f. The fee of a grain measurer.
subs: Come! come! (a mode of calling pigeons, falcons, &c.).

def: n. m. 1. Wrangle; quarrel ( jhagṛā ). 2. Pain; anguish; grief.
more: s. m. Disease, sickness, ail, ailment, pain, anguish.
s. m. A hunter, a fowler.

def: s.f. Wind ( = bayār).
more: n. f. S. वा to move. 1. Wind. 2. An evil spirit. 3. A serpent. Basahā chaṛhāl Shiv byāhan chalale lihale bhari phāṅkā bhāṅg, Āṭho āṅg Shiv byāl lapeṭal dunoṅ hāthe lihalīnh sāṅgi . Bhoj. Shiv marriage song. Shiv on a bull went forth to wed, gulping great lumps of bhang , His trident in his hand, his body all with serpents hung.

def: imperat. (2nd. pers. sing. of āvardan), Bring thou.
s.m. (?), Land cultivated to be sown in the following year.
s.m. Separation (esp. of lovers); unrequited love ( = bijog).
more: n. m. S. वियोग Separation (generally of lovers); unrequited love. Bābū, jaise dihale mor Sarwan sog, Oise paṛhū toro Rām biog! Mag. song. Sarwan. As you my Sarwan wounded have, Sir, May you from Ram be cast off ever!
s. m. Separation, absence (especially, of lovers). See بجوگ bi-jog.

def: s.m. A lover suffering the pain of absence.
more: n. m. A lover suffering the pain of absence.
s. m. A lover suffering the pain of absence.

def: s.f. Merchandise; an article of merchandise; stock-in-trade, capital; agency, commission.
more: s. f. Capital stock in trade.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بِضَاعَت (bizā'at), from Arabic بِضَاعَة (biḍāʕa).

def: s.m. General slaughter
more: n. m. زدن To kill. A general slaughter ( bighan .) Nādir Shāh ne Dillī meṅ ḍhāī ghaṛī tak bizan kīyā thā .
imper. of the verb زدن Strike. ¶ bazan, s. A harrow or instrument for levelling ploughed land.

def: adv. By its own essence; of itself; of himself; in person, personally; essentially; independently; intrinsically.
more: adv. Of himself; of itself; in person ( asālatan ); absolutely; ex necessitate rei. bi-zātihī zimmeh-dār . At his personal debit; personally liable or responsible.
adv. By its own essence, of itself, of himself, in person, personally, essentially, independently, intrinsically.

more: s. m. A garment for daily use of which no care is taken.

def: adv. Of necessity, necessarily, inevitably, perforce; of course, certainly, positively.

more: 1. (from بونا) v. a. To cause to sow. 2. (from بو Smell) v. n. To stink, to emit a smell, to smell.

def: s.m. The sowing of grain by the drill.
s.m. Goods and chattels, property; bundle.
more: s. m. 1. Goods and chattels, property. 2. A bundle.

def: s.m. Alligator, crocodile (syn. magar; kumbhīr).
more: E. n. m. An alligator ( magar ).
s. m. An alligator. See کنبهير.

ety: from S. rt. कुच् with अव
def: s.m. A chair-pālkī, a kind of sedan (syn. jhappān).
adj. Chipped, jagged, notched (on the edge), serrated, denticulated; gapped (as the teeth).
s.m. (f.?), Distance, remoteness (syn. dūrī).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian بعد (ba'd), from Arabic بَعْد (baʕd).

def: adj. Weak, feeble; soft, faint-hearted, low-spirited, timid; low, mean, trifling, trivial, worthless; — s.m. A buffalo.
more: adj. 1. Weak; not strong; feeble. Maiṅ aisā bodā nahīh hūṅ jo tum se dab jāūṅ . 2. Faint-hearted; timid; cowardly. 3. Trifling; trivial; worthless; low; mean.

def: s.m. A square enclosure of mud in which water is taken for irrigation; a a place to stand on for throwing the daurī or basket by which water is raised from a well.
1. adj. Scented. 2. s. m. A kind of leather so called.
adj. Intelligent, sensible, ingenious.
s.m. Understanding, knowledge, wisdom, intelligence; perception, apprehension, cognition, conception, thought.
s.m. A security.
n. m. S. बुध् to know. 1. Knowledge. See بُده (1, 2.). 2. The religion of Buddhā.
adj. & s.m. Causing to know, informing, teaching; — teacher; informer, spy.
s.m. Perception by the understanding, comprehension; knowledge; teaching, informing; arousing, awakening; — incense burning.
s.f. = bodhan, q.v. s.m. Perception by the understanding, comprehension; knowledge; teaching, informing; arousing, awakening; — incense burning.
part. Made known, taught; expressed; apprized.

ety: bodh˚ = Prk. बोधे(इ), or बोध(इ) = S. बोधय(ति), caus. of rt. बुध्
def: v.t. To learn, to commit to memory; — to wheedle, cajole, coax; to flatter (syn. phuslānā; bhulānā).
more: v. a. 1. To learn by heart; commit to memory. 2. To wheedle; cajole; coax ( phuslānā ). E.

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Simple, artless, innocent, soft, silly, foolish; — simpleton, a soft; — bodlī, s.f. Simple woman, trollop; a woman who lives with a be-nawā faqīr, and dresses like a man.
1. adj. f. A simple woman, a trollop. 2. s. f. A woman who lives with a be-nawā faḳīr and dresses like a man.
s.m. Outer covering, wrapper; pouch

more: s. m. The cloth in which any thing (particularly, a saddle) is wrapped up.

more: 1. s. m. Trumpery, trifles, scraps, rags, &c. the stores of a wallet. 2. adj. f. Ugly and fat (woman).

more: s. m. A dish made of boiled meat, flour, &c. بوغرانکلنا bog̠ẖrā nikalnā, To be well beaten.

def: v.t. To set afloat ( = bahānā, q.v.); to send away; to separate.

more: s. f. The first sale for ready money in the morning.

def: s.f. = bohnī, q.v. s.f. Handsel; first sale for ready money early in the day (considered as a good omen and as determining the luck of the day. — The etymology is doubtful: some trace the word to bhawānī, because ascribed to the auspices of that goddess; others derive it from the root vr̤idh).

n. f. H. behvār transaction, or S. वृध् to increase. Hansel; the first money received during the day, or the first ready-money sale by shopkeepers or hucksters, no credit being given as a rule for the article first sold. ☞ The practice says Elliot, "is universal in India, and is precisely like the handsel of England," which Lemon in his Dictionary, explains to be, "the first money received at market, which many superstitious people will spit on, either to render it tenacious that it may remain with them and not vanish away like a fairy gift, or else to render it propitious and lucky that it may draw more money to it." 1. Pahlī bohnī, Allāh miyāṅ kī ās . Prov. The first money is to hand, for the rest my hope is in God. 2. Bohnī ṭhonī, rad balā . Prov. The first sale made, ill luck hath fled.
s.m. A man who (having no plough of his own) works with a borrowed plough, spade, &c.

ety: S. व्यवहारकः, rt. हृ, with वि and अअव
def: s.m. A class of village bankers or money-lenders; an individual of that class. (The Bohrās are Mohammadans, who came originally from Guzrāt)
more: n. m. H. behwār transaction. A village banker or money lender. The Bohras appear to have originated in Guzrat, where they became converts to Mahamadanism, but they are settled in many parts of Central and Western India and in the N. W. Provinces.

more: adj. 1. Loaded; laden. 2. Heavy; ponderous; massive. bojhoṅ marnā , v. n. 1. To groan and sweat under a burden. 2. To give a lame reply or weak retort. ' Jāṭ re jāṭ, tere sir pe khāṭ.' 'Telī re telī, tere sir pe kolū .' Prov. ' Tuk nahīṅ milā!' 'Bojhoṅ to maregā! ' 'Jat, ho! a bed upon your head.' 'Ho! oilman, ho! oil-press upon your head!' 'That's no rhyme.' 'What then?
